message:
🌱 Add kyverno: [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing mission (#1053)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kyverno-9930-bug-kyverno-processes-all-pods-in-a-mutate-existing-namespaced-poli",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kyverno: [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing Namespaced Policy",
+    "description": "[Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing Namespaced Policy. This issue affects 11+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kyverno troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kyverno deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kyverno configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
Kyverno appears to perform some level of processing of all Pods across the cluster when matching against a Namespaced Policy where the `match` block does not specify a Namespace and the rule is a mutate existing rule."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing…",
+        "description": "*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

### Proof Manifests
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
  name: kyverno:update-pods
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - ""
  resources:
  - pods
  verbs:
  - update
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

### Proof Manifests",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
  name: kyverno:update-pods
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - ""
  resources:
  - pods
  verbs:
  - update",
+        "apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: Policy
metadata:
  name: label-pod-test-1
  namespace: default
spec:
  mutateExistingOnPolicyUpdate: true
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: add-label
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          names:
          - "test-1-*"
    mutate:
      targets:
      - apiVersion: v1
        kind: Pod
        name: "{{ request.object.metadata.name }}"
        namespace: default
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            my-label: "true"",
+        "# Kyverno CLI test manifest (please see docs for latest manifest format at https://kyverno.io/docs/kyverno-cli/). See kyverno/policies for complete examples of all related test files."
+      ]
+    }
+  },
